title: Trade-offs between enzyme fitness and solubility illuminated by deep mutational scanning
research_objectives: Characterize trade-offs between enzyme solubility and catalytic activity
Evaluate sequence determinants of protein solubility
Develop predictive models for solubility-enhancing mutations maintaining fitness
Identify design principles for improving enzyme stability without sacrificing activity

research_scope.focus_area: Protein solubility and enzyme fitness trade-offs in protein engineering
research_scope.methodology: Deep mutational scanning with complementary selection systems

biophysical_fundamentals.solubility_definition: Probability of proper folding upon translation

problem_statement.engineering_challenge: Mutations increasing solubility often decrease catalytic activity
problem_statement.consequence: Requires laborious high-throughput activity screens to identify good variants
problem_statement.need: Generalizable method to predict beneficial solubility mutations maintaining fitness

experimental_design.deep_mutational_scanning: Assess nearly all possible single point mutations
complementary_selections: Yeast surface display (YSD) screening for solubility
experimental_design.fitness_comparison: Compare results with published experimental fitness landscapes

solubility_mutation_frequency.alignment: Matches theoretical predictions of global protein stability

predictive_modeling.training: Based on experimentally observed fitness landscapes
predictive_modeling.accuracy: 90% for predicting solubility-enhancing mutations maintaining fitness
predictive_modeling.limitation: Removal of rare mutations improving both fitness and solubility

design_strategies.back_to_consensus: Select mutations oversampled in evolutionary history
design_strategies.advantage: Preserves enzyme function while improving stability
design_strategies.active_site_avoidance: Avoid mutations near active site when possible

structural_characterization.sample: LGK mutant with improved solubility and function
structural_characterization.purpose: Reveal biophysical basis of stability-activity trade-offs

biotechnology_applications.biocatalysis: Improved solubility increases catalytic lifetime and turnover
biotechnology_applications.expression: Higher yields of soluble, active enzyme
biotechnology_applications.therapeutics: Better formulation stability and shelf life
biotechnology_applications.manufacturing: Enhanced production of enzymes for specialty chemicals
